title: AC Tulare — Full Show
description: Full show for AC Tulare, a typeface I designed.

title: Clearly Magic — Excerpt
description: Excerpt from Clearly Magic, an essay I wrote on the relationship between technology, complexity, and enchantment through the lens of transparent electronics.
content: If the awe of transparent electronics is an aestheticization of a domesticated and simultaneously mystified complexity, then as a consumer product, they appease a desire to claim—to literally own—an embodiment of that obscurity without the cost of having to master it. This is an ethical position. It is saying that we should not need nor want to know how our technology works, save only that we own them.

title: AC Tulare — Field Notes Type Specimen
It's Nice That
description: Excerpt from It's Nice That feature.
content: “I grew to become very sensitive to the ways language and design engendered certain modes of interfacing and interpreting the world, which made design and language not only an issue of power, but an inherently political activity too,” says Aldon. “This is where my ethical and aesthetic affinities correspond, it’s why I think I tend to gravitate towards designing systems of some kind: interfaces, tools and more.”

title: Comparator Mirror
description: An original design of a 19th century painting tool based on the camera obscura.

title: Creative Lives In Progress
description: Excerpt from interview with Creative Lives in Progress.
content: I used to joke that I was a liberal arts student trapped in a design program because my greatest influences so far have come from outside the design tradition. Maggie Nelson’s writing and criticism had an enormous impact on me growing up, as did Chris Kraus’ novels, Ursula Le Guin’s essays and Ludwig Wittgenstein’s ideas. I think a lot of my work and ethical perspective, especially when I was a student, had traces of their influence. The liberal arts taught me to understand design as situated within a larger continuum of human thought, which made it genuinely meaningful to me as a way of engaging with issues that extend far beyond design itself.
